t_ch: 你在干嘛？
t_en: What are you doing?

t_ch: 啊？我在写诗。你没看见嘛，那边的桌面上已经摆了好几版了。
t_en: Oh, I am just writing a poem. Can't you see the drafts on the desk?

t_ch: 唉，你这一打断倒是提醒我，都写了这么多版了，莱斯莉怎么还不给我回信呢？
t_en: Oh, your interruption reminds me. How come Lesley still hasn't replied to my letters after all those poems I sent her?

t_ch: 你看过我写给莱斯莉的那些诗了吧，怎么样？
t_en: You've read the poems I sent Lesley. What do you think?

t_ch: 都是些酸诗。
t_en: They are pretty cringy.

t_ch: 臭不可闻。
t_en: They stink.

t_ch: 别理我师父，这些诗完全抒发了我的内心所想。你一看就跟空间站里死气沉沉的普通科员不同，肯定能够理解我的风情。
t_en: Don't mind my master. These poems speak my mind. You don't look like those dull researchers around the space station. I'm sure you'd understand how I feel.

t_ch: 我还没认真看过…
t_en: I haven't looked...

t_ch: 啊，真是不凑巧。有空翻翻看吧，你要是懂这玩意儿，还能帮我推敲推敲。
t_en: Oh, that's unfortunate. Take a look when you have time. Give me some suggestions if you know a thing or two.

t_ch: 都是些酸诗罢了。
t_en: They are just cringy.

t_ch: 你和我师父一样的看法？看来写诗对我这种大老粗还是太难了……
t_en: What? You share the same view as my master? Looks like writing poems is too hard for a rough fellow like me...

t_ch: 写得挺有意思。
t_en: They are pretty interesting.

t_ch: 是吧！他们都嫌我不务正业，只有你懂得里面的情趣。说说看！你喜欢哪一版？
t_en: I know! They all say I am wasting my time. You are the only one who understands the fun of it. So tell me, which is your favorite?

t_ch: 莉莉，我的莉莉…
t_en: Ley, oh, my Ley...

t_ch: 嘿嘿，看来你也喜欢打直球。
t_en: Heheh, you also prefer to be direct.

t_ch: 不过我担心这一版写得太直接了，贸然发出去，会暴露我炙热的感情，给她带来很大压力。
t_en: But I am worried this may be too direct. If I send this to her, it will reveal my burning desire and put her under tremendous pressure.

t_ch: 从黑塔离开…
t_en: You were as light as a swallow when leaving the station...

t_ch: 哈，你也喜欢这一版！他们都说这一版用词太过粗鄙，没想到你才是我的知音。
t_en: Ha, you like this one too! They all say my choice of words is too rough. It turns out you know me best!

t_ch: 他们哪懂得语词不必多精致，唯有真情才是诗的灵魂。写这一版我只花了十分钟！
t_en: They don't understand that the soul of poetry lies in real emotions, rather than beautiful words. It only took me <unbreak>10</unbreak> minutes to write this!

t_ch: 除了巨环与星芒外…
t_en: Other than the halo and starlight...

t_ch: 原来你喜欢这种风格啊。不过，我担心被她拒绝才写的这一版……
t_en: Oh, so you're into that style. But I only wrote this in case she rejects me...

t_ch: 希望最后别用上吧。
t_en: I hope I don't have to use it in the end.

t_ch: 但是就像我刚才说的，这诗写得怎么样不重要，重要的是要有机会把诗送出去……
t_en: But just like what I said, it doesn't matter how these poems are. First I need to be able to send them out...

t_ch: 所以，修复中继器的事情就拜托你啦。
t_en: So, please help me fix the repeater.

t_ch: 莱斯莉是…
t_en: Who is Lesley...

t_ch: 呵，人家莱斯莉，那可是「黑塔•光渊密卷科」的明星科员，本琥珀纪里唯一出使丰饶星域的访问学者。
t_en: Oh, Lesley is a prominent researcher in the Department of Insight, the only scholar who has visited the Abundance segmentum this Amber Era.

t_ch: 能有空搭理你？
t_en: You sure she has time for you?

t_ch: 当然有空搭理我！自从莱斯莉走后，我们一直断断续续地通过银河电波中继器联系。
t_en: Of course she does! Ever since Lesley left, we've been communicating on and off through the Cosmic Airwave Repeater.

t_ch: 这里的人也会写信？
t_en: Do people here write letters too?

t_ch: 哦，你别误会，我说的不是纸质信件。自从莱斯莉走后，我们一直断断续续地通过银河电波中继器联系。
t_en: Oh, don't get me wrong. I was not talking about paper letters. Ever since Lesley left, we've been communicating on and off through the Cosmic Airwave Repeater.

t_ch: 即使隔着数万光年的距离，她也会回复我的消息，只是这次……
t_en: Even if we are tens of thousands of light-years apart, she still replies to my messages. Only this time...

t_ch: 人家可是「黑塔•光渊密卷科」的明星科员，本琥珀纪里唯一出使丰饶星域的访问学者。
t_en: She is a prominent researcher at the Department of Insight, the only scholar who has visited the Abundance segmentum in this Amber Era.

t_ch: 科考工作忙得很，还指望次次都给你回复哪？
t_en: She must be very busy with her research. Don't expect her to keep answering you all the time!

t_ch: 她没有按时回信，应该只是这次情况特殊罢了！
t_en: This is the only time she hasn't replied to me.

t_ch: 我、我写了信和莱斯莉表白，不像平时，只是告诉她地概科有多少种石头、泥巴……
t_en: I... I confessed my love for her in my last letter. I usually just talk to her about the types of rock and mud we see in Department of Cosmic Geography...

t_ch: 臭小子。
t_en: Brat.

t_ch: 对自己的学科放尊重一点！
t_en: Show some respect to your own discipline, will you?

t_ch: 我真的很不理解，为什么偏偏在我表白之后，莱斯莉就不理我了……
t_en: I really don't understand why Lesley stopped answering me after I confessed my love for her...

t_ch: 我们曾经聊了那么多。从密卷科<unbreak>101</unbreak>个星神密仪中有哪些是需要用到古恩的胡子，聊到……
t_en: We used to chat about a lot of things, from which of the <unbreak>101</unbreak> Aeon instruments in the Department of Insight require Gunn's beard, to...

t_ch: 我们从密卷科<unbreak>101</unbreak>个星神密仪中有哪些是需要用到古恩的胡子，聊到……
t_en: We talk about everything. We talk about which of the <unbreak>101</unbreak> Aeon instruments in the Department of Insight would require Gunn's beard, and...

t_ch: 勘星钎该如何「相土石」，才能通过考察土质好坏来判断某个星球神眷的旺衰。
t_en: And about how to use Astro Rods to examine the quality of soil, thus determining the rise and fall of the inhabitants of the planet.

t_ch: 兄弟，你还挺浪漫。
t_en: Bro, you are very romantic.

t_ch: 这就叫浪漫？那我们聊的「浪漫」可多了。
t_en: So this is romantic? Then we had even more "romantic" discussions.

t_ch: 不回复就是一种婉拒…
t_en: Ghosting you might just be her way of rejection.

t_ch: 不可能，要是她想拒绝我，还能和我聊这么多？更何况……
t_en: That's impossible. If she were to reject me, why would she talk to me so much? Besides...

t_ch: 哪有人和女孩聊泥巴啊。
t_en: Who the hell talks about mud with a girl...

t_ch: 泥巴…泥巴怎么啦？！即便是和莱斯莉聊泥巴，那也是相当有意思。更何况……
t_en: Mud... what's wrong with mud!? With Lesley, it's fun even when I talk about mud. Besides...

t_ch: 在我表白前的那次通讯中，她还向我描述了一场壮丽非常的<color=#dbc291ff>星震</color>。
t_en: During the communication where I confessed my love, she told me about a spectacular <color=#dbc291ff>starquake</color>.

t_ch: 你想想，那是恒、星、衰、亡！别人躲都躲不及，可我的莉莉，不仅获得了第一手的观测资料，甚至顺手救了一名死里逃生的银河搭车客。
t_en: Think about it! A star DIED! Most people would try to hide, but not my Lesley. Not only did she get first-hand observational data, she even saved a cosmic hitchhiker who almost died!

t_ch: 你是不知道，这封复信里她的语气有多快乐，连中继器的信号都那么快乐！
t_en: You have no idea how happy she sounded in her reply. Even the Airwave Repeater signal was cheerful!

t_ch: 每次收到她的回信，我都会情不自禁地想象着，拿一颗石头雕出她的笑脸。
t_en: Whenever I receive a reply from her, I can't help but picture the smile on her face and I'd carve it on a rock.

t_ch: 「军团」入侵前，我的桌子上已经放了整整一排这样的小石头。可惜，它们在混乱中全丢了。
t_en: Before the Legion invasion, I had a bunch of rocks all lined up in a row on my desk. Unfortunately, they went missing in the chaos.

t_ch: 风月私情。
t_en: Reckless romance.

t_ch: 有害学术！
t_en: It's detrimental to your research!

t_ch: 总之，没有任何预兆表明她会拒绝我的表白。所以，如果我没有在约定时间收到她的消息…一定是银河电波中继器出了问题！
t_en: Anyway, there is no sign that she's going to reject me. If I don't receive her reply within the usual time frame... it means there must be something wrong with the Cosmic Airwave Repeater!

t_ch: 我去调查过，有一帮「军团」怪物占领了收发讯号的区域，你能帮我去看看到底出了事吗？
t_en: In my investigation, I found out that a group of Legion monsters occupied the area where the signals are transmitted. Can you help me find out what happened?

t_ch: 你能帮我去看看中继器到底出了啥毛病吗？师父他不让我去……
t_en: Can you help me find out what happened to the Airwave Repeater? My master wouldn't let me go...

t_ch: 你不准帮他的忙！
t_en: Don't help him!

t_ch: 满脑子就是中继器中继器，十几天没干正经事。不争气！
t_en: All he thinks about is the Airwave Repeater. He hasn't done any proper work for more than <unbreak>10</unbreak> days. Shame on you!

t_ch: 我帮你去看看~
t_en: I'll help you.

t_ch: 老人家，堵不如疏嘛。
t_en: We may as well get this off his chest.

t_ch: 你不争气！
t_en: Shame on you!

t_ch: 太好了！
t_en: Nice!

t_ch: 唉！你们这帮小崽子……
t_en: Hmph! A bunch of hooligans...

t_ch: 别听我师父乱说！你要是有空听他唠叨，早找着银河电波中继器啦。
t_en: Don't listen to my master! If you have the time to listen to him, you would've found the Cosmic Airwave Repeater already.

t_ch: 听我讲，<color=#dbc291ff>中继器</color>就在<color=#dbc291ff>「嘘实埋间」</color>的旁边！那是一座大型的废弃奇物仓库。
t_en: Listen, the <color=#dbc291ff>Airwave Repeater</color> is next to the <color=#dbc291ff>Disposal Site</color>! It is a massive warehouse for storing discarded Curios.

t_ch: 要是成功修复我们的通讯，我一定报答你——早晚请你当{F#伴娘}{M#伴郎}！
t_en: If you can resume our communication, I promise I'll reward you by inviting you to be our {F#bridesmaid}{M#best man}!

t_ch: 即使隔着数万光年的距离，她也不会不理我的。
t_en: She will never ignore me even though we are tens of thousands of light-years away.

t_ch: 异常！线路异常！已启动解离保护程序。
t_en: Error! Abnormal route! Detachment protection activated.

t_ch: 侦测到——超镍零件缺失。
t_en: Detected — Ultra Nickel Parts Missing.

t_ch: 超镍零件…看来中继器也有点年头了……
t_en: Ultra Nickel Parts... this Airwave Repeater is quite old...

t_ch: 刚好旁边就是存放废弃奇物的「嘘实埋间」，说不定有能用的替换件。
t_en: Luckily it is right next to the Disposal Site where discarded Curios are stored. Maybe I can find some spare parts.

t_ch: 哔嘟！检测到超镍零件，启动归位复机程序。
t_en: *BEEP* Ultra Nickel Parts detected. Initiating recovery procedure.

t_ch: ~电~波~已~上~线~
t_en: AIRWAVE IS ONLINE.

t_ch: 尚未侦测到正在传输的电波讯号。如有需要，请选择您要察看的过往讯号转译纪录。
t_en: Transmitting airwave has not been detected. If needed, please select the historical signal decoding record that you wish to review.

t_ch: <unbreak>40</unbreak>月<unbreak>-43</unbreak>月
t_en: <unbreak>2157</unbreak> AE, Months <unbreak>40</unbreak>–<unbreak>43</unbreak>

t_ch: <unbreak>44</unbreak>月<unbreak>-47</unbreak>月
t_en: <unbreak>2157</unbreak> AE, Months <unbreak>44</unbreak>–<unbreak>47</unbreak>

t_ch: <unbreak>48</unbreak>月<unbreak>-51</unbreak>月
t_en: <unbreak>2157</unbreak> AE, Months <unbreak>48</unbreak>–<unbreak>51</unbreak>

t_ch: 下一页。
t_en: Next page.

t_ch: 请选择您要察看的过往讯号转译纪录。
t_en: You can view the historical signal decoding record here.

t_ch: <unbreak>52</unbreak>月<unbreak>-55</unbreak>月
t_en: <unbreak>2157</unbreak> AE, Months <unbreak>52</unbreak>–<unbreak>55</unbreak>

t_ch: 本时间段无电波讯号转译纪录。
t_en: No signal decoding record was found during this period.

t_ch: 发生甚么事了？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 收发频率变慢了。
t_en: Is there a delay in signal transmission?

t_ch: 莱斯莉没回信吗…
t_en: Did Lesley not reply...

t_ch: <unbreak>56</unbreak>月-当前
t_en: <unbreak>2157</unbreak> AE, Months <unbreak>56</unbreak>–present

t_ch: 傻洛奇，我当然明白你的意思。至于我的回答……
t_en: Rocky, how silly of you, of course I understand what you mean. As for my answer...

t_ch: （该纪录已损坏。）
t_en: (Record is damaged.)

t_ch: 结束纪录查阅。
t_en: End record lookup.

t_ch: 历史纪录查阅结束。尚未侦测到正在传输的电波讯…号……
t_en: End of historical record lookup. Airwave Transmission has not been detected.

t_ch: 纪录在这时候损坏？！
t_en: Why was the record damaged here?

t_ch: 恋爱真好啊。
t_en: Sweet, sweet love.

t_ch: 坏机器…坏…坏……
t_en: Stupid machine... stupid... stupid...

t_ch: 无法识别，无法识别，检测到生物情绪波动。
t_en: Cannot identify. Cannot identify. Lifeform emotional fluctuation detected.

t_ch: 警告！您已进入「黑塔」全景监控区，请勿对中继器做出不当举动。
t_en: Warning! You've entered the full surveillance area of the space station. Please do not conduct any inappropriate behavior toward the repeater.

t_ch: ~电~波~已~下~线~
t_en: AIRWAVE IS OFFLINE.

t_ch: 关键纪录损坏…全景监控区……
t_en: Key record is damaged... full surveillance area...

t_ch: 去监控室调查看看有没有线索…看起来像是有人对中继器动了手脚……
t_en: I better check the surveillance in the monitoring room... it looks like someone has done something to the repeater...

t_ch: 您好，权限验证…已通过。
t_en: Hello, authentication... passed.

t_ch: 请选择要察看的监控区域。
t_en: Please choose the surveillance area you want to review.

t_ch: 主控舱段轮岗亭。
t_en: Master control zone shift station.

t_ch: （不是这里。）
t_en: (Not here)

t_ch: 基座舱段安全区。
t_en: Base zone safe zone.

t_ch: （不是这里。）
t_en: (Not here)

t_ch: 收容舱段安全区。
t_en: Storage zone safe zone.

t_ch: （不是这里。）
t_en: (Not here)

t_ch: 下一页。
t_en: Next page.

t_ch: 请选择要察看的监控区域。
t_en: Please choose the surveillance area you want to review.

t_ch: 机修间。
t_en: Maintenance Room.

t_ch: （不是这里。）
t_en: (Not here)

t_ch: 嘘实埋间。
t_en: Disposal Site.

t_ch: 虽然监控只拍到了一个模糊的背影，但他好像是…伯纳德？！
t_en: The camera only captured the image of a blurry figure from behind, it looks like... Bernard!?

t_ch: 医疗舱。
t_en: Medical cabin.

t_ch: （不是这里。）
t_en: (Not here)

t_ch: 察看结束。
t_en: End of observation.

t_ch: 操作结束，再见。
t_en: Operation ended. Goodbye.

t_ch: 没想到监控还拍到了艾丝妲，以及两个少见的少年科员……
t_en: I can't believe the surveillance camera captured Asta and two rarely-seen young researchers...

t_ch: 但需要调查的区域不是这里。
t_en: But this is not the area I should be investigating.

t_ch: 监控拍到的这段时间，接待大厅似乎尚未设立安全区。
t_en: During the time captured by the camera, the safe zone in the Reception Hall hasn't been set up.

t_ch: 不见奇物再收容小队，只有「军团」游荡的余孽。
t_en: The Curio Collection Squad is nowhere to be seen, only the wandering monsters of the Legion.

t_ch: 这不是三月七和丹恒吗？两人步履匆匆，当时急着去干嘛呢……
t_en: Isn't that March 7th, and Dan Heng? They looked like they were in a hurry to do something...

t_ch: 算了，别多想，现在最要紧是找到中继器的监控记录。
t_en: Forget it. The most important thing is to find the surveillance record of the repeater.

t_ch: 这显然是有人被困在斥力桥时所摄影像。
t_en: This surveillance footage clearly shows that someone is trapped in the repulsion bridge.

t_ch: 终于被我找到了。
t_en: Found it, at last.

t_ch: 受伤的科员正在医疗舱内接受治疗……
t_en: The injured researcher is under medical treatment in the medical cabin.

t_ch: 既然知道是谁了，现在，就去找他对质吧。
t_en: Now that I know who that is, let me confront him and find out the truth.

t_ch: …听不懂你在说什么。
t_en: ...I don't know what you're talking about.

t_ch: 是你干的吧？
t_en: It was you, wasn't it?

t_ch: 年轻人不学习，净知道瞎转悠。
t_en: Young people should spend more time studying instead of wandering around.

t_ch: 监控都拍到了。
t_en: It's been captured by the security footage.

t_ch: …看来你把机器修好了。
t_en: ...It looks like you've fixed the machine.

t_ch: 为什么破坏纪录？
t_en: Why did you damage the record?

t_ch: …说得这么精确，看来你有证据。
t_en: Don't jump to conclusions. Show me your evidence.

t_ch: 那我去告诉洛奇。
t_en: I'll tell Rocky then.

t_ch: …等一下。
t_en: Hold on.

t_ch: 唉！你们这些小崽子，真是让人操碎了心！
t_en: Hmph! Thanks to you kids, I am worried to death!

t_ch: ——我破坏纪录，那都是为了洛奇好！他为了莱斯莉，整天不学无术…这就算了。
t_en: I damaged the record for Rocky's own good! He's forgone his research because of that Lesley... Not to mention...

t_ch: 更别提你们对事情根本缺乏了解，莱斯莉她……
t_en: Not to mention you have no idea what's happening right now. Lesley...
Lesley...

t_ch: 答应了？
t_en: Did she say yes?

t_ch: 对。她答应了洛奇的表白。
t_en: Yes, she said yes.

t_ch: 拒绝了？
t_en: Did she say no?

t_ch: 不。她答应了洛奇的表白。
t_en: No, she said yes.

t_ch: 出事了？
t_en: Did something happen to her?

t_ch: 没有，她毫发无伤。而且，她答应了洛奇的表白。
t_en: No, she is safe and sound. And she said yes to Rocky's confession.

t_ch: 但是他们不能在一起。
t_en: But they can't be together.

t_ch: 如果你以为是我从中作梗，那你就错了！小崽子，自作聪明。
t_en: If you think I am the one being difficult here, you are wrong! Don't get ahead of yourself!

t_ch: 他们不能在一起…永远不可能在一起。
t_en: They can't be together... they will never be together.

t_ch: 你知道为什么吗？不，你不知道。很少有人明白星震到底是怎么一回事……
t_en: Do you know why? No, you don't. People rarely understand what a starquake means...

t_ch: 如果防护得当，星震不会对人产生物理性伤害。
t_en: With proper protection, a starquake will not cause physical damage to humans.

t_ch: 但是，星震造成的重力坍塌，会使所在星域的时空曲率发生巨大变化。
t_en: However, the gravitational collapse caused by a starquake will severely change the space-time curvature of the segmentum.

t_ch: 打个比方，莱斯莉所在星域的时间，曾经和我们一样，是一条直线，那么如今，它已经被重力压成了一道巨大的峡谷。
t_en: For example, the time in Lesley's segmentum was once a straight line like ours. But now, it has been pressed into a huge canyon by gravity.

t_ch: 显而易见，曲线总是长于直线。所以她所在星域的时光曲线，当折算至我们的时间直线时，会覆盖若干倍的长度。
t_en: Clearly, a curve is always longer than a straight line. Therefore, the time curve of her segmentum is going to cover several times the length when converted to our timeline.

t_ch: 你知不知道，这意味着什么？
t_en: Do you even know what that means?

t_ch: 时间流速不一。
t_en: The flow of time will be different.

t_ch: 完全没听懂…
t_en: I don't get it...

t_ch: 她成了神仙？
t_en: Has she become a god?

t_ch: 这意味着我们和她的时间流速不一致。
t_en: It means the velocity of our time is different from hers.

t_ch: 打从星震发生后，莱斯莉与我们的时间差将逐步增大。她发送复信的时间，会从一个月，变成一年，再延长到十年，都有可能。
t_en: Since the starquake, the temporal difference between Lesley's and ours will gradually increase. It is possible that the time it takes for her reply to arrive will change from one month to a year, and maybe even ten years.

t_ch: 不光是消息，她的任何活动都是如此。在她离开异常的重力环境之前，她在银河中每跋涉一个月，都将耗费我们数十年的等待。
t_en: This isn't just about her message, but everything she does. Until she leaves that abnormal gravity environment, every month she spends among the cosmos will equal decades for us.

t_ch: 如果说洛奇，包括我们在内，正生活在「现在」这样的此在之点。
t_en: Let's say, if Rocky, including us, is living at a point called "the present."

t_ch: 那么相对来说，莱斯莉她……
t_en: Then in comparison, Lesley...

t_ch: 她已经活在了「未来」。
t_en: She is living in "the future."

t_ch: …我也曾是个小伙子。
t_en: ...I used to be a young man once.

t_ch: 我想，比起被拒绝，「本该」却「无法」和心意相通的人在一起，那才是更为残酷的事情。
t_en: In my opinion, not being able to be with someone you love is even more cruel than being rejected.

t_ch: 也许还有办法…
t_en: Maybe there are other ways...

t_ch: 没有什么办法。
t_en: There aren't any.

t_ch: 至少答应过他。
t_en: At least tell him.

t_ch: 那又怎么样？
t_en: And then what?

t_ch: 不该由你决定！
t_en: It's not up to you to decide!

t_ch: 所以你想告诉他真相。但请你告诉我，他又能做什么？
t_en: If you want to tell him the truth, then tell me — what can he do?

t_ch: 知道真相后，洛奇无非要用数十倍的时间，等一条本该很快返回的讯息。
t_en: After knowing the truth, Rocky will have to wait ten times longer for a message that should have arrived right away.

t_ch: 无非孤独地生活在此刻，等着那个有莱斯莉的未来，一步一步朝他走来。
t_en: He would have to live in this lonely present and wait for the future containing Lesley to finally come to him.

t_ch: 但他这辈子能等多久？
t_en: But how much time can he spend waiting?

t_ch: 也许当他垂垂老矣，成了我这样的老头子，莱斯莉终于回到了「黑塔」。
t_en: Maybe Lesley will finally return to the space station when he is old and decrepit like me.

t_ch: 但从她身上流过的时间是那么少，或许她还和现在一般无二年纪。
t_en: But the flow of time for her is so slow. Maybe, at that time, she will still be as young as she is today.

t_ch: 那时候，他们该怎么在一起？
t_en: When that time comes, how are they going to be together?

t_ch: 空间，可以逾越；但时间，触不可及！
t_en: We may transcend space, but time is beyond our reach!

t_ch: 你们都还太年轻…还不知道，这世间但凡需要等待的事，一般没有结果。
t_en: You are way too young... you don't understand. Things that require a lot of waiting don't usually end up well.

t_ch: 不撞南墙不回头。
t_en: He will never give up if he doesn't know the truth.

t_ch: 还是别告诉他了。
t_en: We better not tell him.

t_ch: 本来，破坏纪录，取走零件，推在怪物身上，可以让洛奇永远寄希望于中继器被修好的那一天。
t_en: At first I thought if I damage the record, take away the parts, and blame everything on the monsters, I could keep Rocky in the hope that, one day the repeater will be repaired.

t_ch: 现在，既然是你揭开了这层纱，就由你来做决定吧。
t_en: Now, since you have found out, the decision is up to you.

t_ch: 我手上有两个从中继器拷贝下来的讯号转译录，分别是红色和蓝色。
t_en: I have two signal logs I copied from the repeater, red and blue.

t_ch: 红色转译录存着莱斯莉的真实回复，但里面包含关于星震的真相——老实说，以洛奇那小崽子的性格，就连我也无法预测他知道真相后会做出什么冲动事。
t_en: The red log contains Lesley's real reply, including the truth about the starquake. To be honest, even I can't imagine what Rocky will do after finding out the truth, given how impulsive he is.

t_ch: 蓝色转译录是我的伪造记录——莱斯莉会拒绝洛奇。别以为这样残忍，以后洛奇就不必再等她的回信，他的人生还有机会。
t_en: The blue log is forged — in which Lesley refuses Rocky. Don't think about this as cruel. This way, Rocky won't have to wait for her reply in the future. He can just move on with his life.

t_ch: 姑且就称它们为红色真相与蓝色谎言吧，由你选择该把哪个转译备份交给他。
t_en: Let's call these "the red truth" and "the blue lie." It's up to you to decide which one you want to give to him.

t_ch: 拿好，仔细地想一想。洛奇还在等你的消息。
t_en: Take them and give it some thought. Rocky is waiting for your news.

t_ch: 哼，年轻人。
t_en: Hmph, naive kids.

t_ch: 哼，我正是这样打算的。
t_en: Hmph, that's also my plan.

t_ch: 哥们儿！你回来啦！你去的这段时间，我又写了一首《咏莱斯莉》。
t_en: Bro, you're back! While you were away, I wrote another poem called "Praise Lesley."

t_ch: 你修好了中继器对吧？我看到你还拿着转译备份…嘿嘿，哪个是我的？
t_en: Did you fix the repeater? I see you are holding decoded backups... Hehe, which one is for me?

t_ch: 红色真相。
t_en: The Red truth.

t_ch: 怎么很犹豫的样子，确定红色是我的吗？
t_en: You seem hesitant. Are you sure the red one is for me?

t_ch: 蓝色谎言。
t_en: The Blue lie.

t_ch: 怎么很犹豫的样子，确定蓝色是我的吗？
t_en: You seem hesitant. Are you sure the blue one is for me?

t_ch: 我确定。
t_en: Yes, I am.

t_ch: 我再想想…
t_en: Let me think...

t_ch: 可~以，不差这一会儿。
t_en: Sure, take your time.

t_ch: 哦。那我拿去读取了，谢啦哥们儿！
t_en: Okay. I'll read it now. Thank you, bro!

t_ch: 怎么会这样…莱斯莉答应了我的表白，但星震那部分……
t_en: I can't believe it... Lesley said yes, but the starquake...

t_ch: 我、我脑子一下子转不过来了……
t_en: I... I can't get my head around this...

t_ch: 似乎是…因为星震的引力效应，她的时间每过去一个月，我的时间就会过去数十年。那岂不是等她回来，我已经像师父一样老了？
t_en: So... Because of the gravitational effect of the starquake, every month she spends in her time will be decades for me. Am I going to be as old as my master when she's back?

t_ch: 是不是这个意思？{NICKNAME}，你再帮我看看，我脑子不灵光，她是这个意思么？
t_en: Is my interpretation correct? {NICKNAME}, help me take a look. I'm a bit slow. Is that what she means?

t_ch: 我想就是这样。
t_en: I think so.

t_ch: 这事不由你控制。
t_en: This is not up to you.

t_ch: 她，去了未来。而我，留在现在…是这样吗？
t_en: She has gone to the future, and I am left in the present... Is this what happened?

t_ch: 那我想念她的漫长时间，对她来说，岂不只是一个毫不起眼的瞬息吗？
t_en: The decades I spend thinking about her will only be a fleeting moment for her, right?

t_ch: 真没想到，连时间也会对人不公平。
t_en: Who would've thought that even time can be so unfair.

t_ch: 所以，指望她回来是不现实了。就算她行进数月，跨越光年，在我的时间坐标里，我已经成了老头一个。
t_en: I guess it's unrealistic to hope that she will come back. Even if she travels for months and crosses light-years, I will have become an old man in my time.

t_ch: 用一辈子的时间去等一个人，忍受无尽的思念与孤独……
t_en: Spending my whole life waiting for someone and enduring endless longing and loneliness...

t_ch: 老实说，哥们儿，你不会真以为我那么天真吧？
t_en: Come on, bro, do you really think I'm that naive?

t_ch: 哈哈！我当然是不会等她的！我不会等！
t_en: Haha! Of course I'm not going to wait for her! I won't!

t_ch: 真没想到你…
t_en: That was unexpected...

t_ch: 明智的决定。
t_en: Smart choice.

t_ch: 我是不会等……
t_en: I am not going to wait for her...

t_ch: 但我要去追她。我要去，追赶时间！
t_en: But I can go after her. I am going to chase after time!

t_ch: 既然她的时空曲线已经成了一座大峡谷，那与其盼着她出来，由我去翻越这座大峡谷不就行了吗？
t_en: Since her time-space curve has become a canyon, wouldn't it be better for me to travel past this canyon instead of waiting for her to come back?

t_ch: 也许我会花十年的时间找到刚度过一个月的她，但我们的时间流速就能从此同频，总比在这里坐以待毙来得好。
t_en: Maybe it will take me ten years to find her, who has only spent a month. But once I do that, our time will be synchronized. It's better than sitting here and doing nothing.

t_ch: 你说是不是？
t_en: Don't you agree?

t_ch: 不，无论{NICKNAME}你怎么看，我都已经决定了！我会尽快跟师父打好招呼，收拾行李，出发去找莱斯莉。
t_en: No, no matter what you think, {NICKNAME}, I've decided! I should inform my master as soon as possible, pack my things, and set off on my journey to find Lesley.

t_ch: 我在这里也早就呆腻了…身为「黑塔•寰宇地概科」的科员，连真正的银河也没见过，说出去早晚笑掉别人的大牙。
t_en: I'm already tired of this place... As a member of the Herta Department of Cosmic Geography, I haven't even seen the real cosmos. Isn't it embarrassing?

t_ch: 我想你肯定和我一样，也很好奇那深空中到底还有何种景象。
t_en: I'm sure you are just as curious as I am about the scenery in deep space.

t_ch: 宇宙里会有什么呢？
t_en: What resides in the deep space?

t_ch: 那里也许危机四伏。
t_en: It may be full of dangers.

t_ch: 我没想过这个问题…
t_en: That never crossed my mind...

t_ch: 壮绝寂寞的奇观，未知美丽的危险，以及我所苦苦追问的那个人…
t_en: Spectacular wonders, enigmatic dangers, and the woman I am chasing after...

t_ch: 也许吧…有未知美丽的危险，也有壮绝寂寞的奇观，以及我所苦苦追问的那个人…
t_en: Maybe... It may be full of spectacular wonders, enigmatic dangers, but it will also have the woman I am chasing after...

t_ch: 至少对我来说，那里有壮绝寂寞的奇观，未知美丽的危险，以及我所苦苦追问的那个人…
t_en: At least for me, there are lonely wonders, the mysterious charm of danger, and the person I am chasing after...

t_ch: 等我找到莱斯莉，总有一天，我会回来告诉你我在这趟旅程所看见的一切……
t_en: When I find Lesley one day, I will come back and tell you everything I witnessed on my journey...

t_ch: 那时，我还要请你当我们的{F#伴娘}{M#伴郎}，你可别忘了这个约定啊。
t_en: By then, I will invite you to be our {F#bridesmaid}{M#best man}. Don't you forget it.

t_ch: 那么，这首诗送给你，再见了，我的朋友。
t_en: Well, this poem is for you. Goodbye, my friend.

t_ch: 黑暗在远方列队。用近乎残忍的方式
t_en: Darkness is gathering in the distance, using cruel methods...

t_ch: 解剖着时间。询问爱情的花瓣已经下坠
t_en: To dissect time. Petals inquiring for love are falling,

t_ch: 如同无限时空中、滴答的钟声。我们别无选择
t_en: Like the ticking chimes in an infinite time and space. We have no choice,

t_ch: 只能滑入宇宙那不详的颤抖，如同滑下一道黄昏的斜坡
t_en: But to slide into the shivering calamities of the universe as if sliding down a slope into dusk.

t_ch: 就这样，群星依旧在头顶赏乐，沿途将是它们优雅的倦容。
t_en: And with that, the stars continue their enjoyment above as their elegant smiles fill the way forward.

t_ch: 哦。那我拿去读取了，谢啦哥们儿！
t_en: Okay. I'll read it now. Thank you, bro!

t_ch: 怎么会这样…莱斯莉，拒绝了我？
t_en: I can't believe it... Lesley rejected me?

t_ch: 可我明明已经很努力了啊…她问的每个问题，我都有问必答。她讲的每个笑话，我都有捧场……
t_en: I tried so hard... I answered all the questions she asked. I laughed at every joke she made...

t_ch: 而且，你也看过那些消息吧？她会关心我，安慰我…难道那些也只是普通的同事情谊吗？
t_en: You've seen our messages, right? She cares about me and she comforts me... is that how colleagues behave?

t_ch: 也许她有苦衷…
t_en: Maybe she has her own reason...

t_ch: 苦衷？她能有什么苦衷…沐浴在神眷里，接触第一手的资料，和杰出的科考队员密切合作……
t_en: Reason? What kind of reason... She's bathing in divine blessings, she receives first-hand information and gets to work with top researchers...

t_ch: 真要有苦衷，那也只能是我…我不知好歹，一直纠缠她。
t_en: If she really has a reason, I am the only one to blame... I've been hassling her without knowing the boundaries...

t_ch: 这不是你的错…
t_en: It's not your fault...

t_ch: 那还会是谁的呢？难道是莱斯莉的错？
t_en: Whose fault is it then? Lesley's?

t_ch: 天涯何处无芳草。
t_en: There's plenty of fish in the sea.

t_ch: 我谁都不在乎，我只在乎莱斯莉。
t_en: I don't care about other women. I only want Lesley.

t_ch: …没想到等了这么久等来这样一条消息。
t_en: ...I can't believe this is what I've been waiting for.

t_ch: 嘿，还不如不让你去修呢，让你看笑话了不是。
t_en: I shouldn't have asked you to fix this. It's so embarrassing.

t_ch: 都怪我太自信。也对，地概科的小科员，黑塔鄙视链的底层，怎么配得上密卷科的明星学者？
t_en: I shouldn't have been so confident. I am only a small potato in the Department of Cosmic Geography, the bottom of the space station's food chain. I must be stupid to think that I'm good enough for a prominent researcher from the Department of Insight.

t_ch: 她身边又是何等角色，汇集了全银河、全宇宙的智囊，我？连吊车尾都排不上。
t_en: Come to think about it, who is she surrounded with? The aggregated wisdom of the entire cosmos, or even the universe. And who am I? I'm not even in the same league.

t_ch: 呵呵…我这副自怨自艾的样子，又要讨师父的骂了。
t_en: Heheh... Master's going to lecture me when he sees me being pathetic again.

t_ch: 睡一觉就好了。
t_en: Get some rest.

t_ch: 嗯，没错，是的，你说得对。
t_en: Yeah, you are right.

t_ch: 只是被拒绝了，对我洛奇来说，也算不上什么大事。实在不行，遇到再来挑衅地概科的人，我就狠狠地揍他们一顿。
t_en: It's just a rejection. It's no big deal to me. If anyone dares to challenge the Department of Cosmic Geography right now, I'm gonna teach them a lesson.

t_ch: 狠狠地…揍他们一顿……
t_en: A good, violent lesson...

t_ch: 这些诗也得藏起来，可不能让他们看见。
t_en: I better hide these poems. I can't let anyone see them.

t_ch: 这些诗写得怪好笑的，是吧？
t_en: They are ridiculous, aren't they?

t_ch: 哥们儿你要是也这么觉得，也别憋着，就笑出来吧。
t_en: Bro, if you want to laugh, laugh. There's no need to hold back.

t_ch: 我眼睛怎么，好像进了点滑石粉…这东西也能弄进眼睛，真滑稽，简直就和我的诗一样滑稽。
t_en: What the heck? Why is there talcum powder in my eyes... Ridiculous, just like my poems.

t_ch: 我先去处理一下，再见了，哥们儿。
t_en: I should go fix it. See you, bro.

t_ch: 课题更重要！
t_en: Your research is more important!

t_ch: 嗯，没错，是的，你说得对。
t_en: Yeah, you are right.

t_ch: 只是被拒绝了，对我洛奇来说，也算不上什么大事。只是可惜了我一大堆的石头，都雕了些什么玩意儿……
t_en: It's just a rejection. It's no big deal to me. But what am I going to do with these rocks? Ridiculous carvings...

t_ch: 勘星钎也送出去了…回头和师父认个错就是了，空间站不也还有一把么，不是什么珍贵的东西……
t_en: I even gave away my Astro Rod... I better go and apologize to my master. There should be another one in the space station. It's not anything expensive anyway...

t_ch: 要把精力放在研究上，毕竟个别人…的认同不重要，重要的是同事们的认可。
t_en: I should shift my focus back to my research. After all, some people's recognition... doesn't matter. Recognition from my colleagues is what matters.

t_ch: 总有一天，总会有人，能看见我洛奇的闪光点。
t_en: One day, one day someone will see my advantages.

t_ch: …是这样的吧？
t_en: ... Am I right?

t_ch: …那个，我眼睛可能进了点滑石粉，没想到，这东西也能弄进眼睛……
t_en: ... Well, some talcum powder got into my eyes. Who would've thought it could get into my eyes...

t_ch: 我先去处理一下，再见了，哥们儿。
t_en: I should go fix it. See you, bro.

t_ch: 没想到你这年轻人，还会回来找我这个老头子。
t_en: I never thought you would visit me again, kid.

t_ch: 来把多余的备份还给你。
t_en: I'm here to return the other backup to you.

t_ch: 有始有终，是个可塑之才。多余的这一份，是蓝色转译录…看来，你还是把真相告诉了他。
t_en: You are someone who finishes what you started. This is the blue log... so you told him the truth.

t_ch: 你也担心洛奇的情况吧…
t_en: You must be worried about Rocky...

t_ch: …我有什么好担心的！路是你们年轻人自己选的，遇到风险就得自己承担，我这种老头子，也只能在旁边干着急。
t_en: Not at all! You choose your own path and suffer your own consequences. An old man like me can do nothing but worry.

t_ch: 你手上剩下的是蓝色转译录…所以洛奇得知了真相，那小崽子现在如何？
t_en: This is the blue log... So Rocky found out the truth. How did he cope with it?

t_ch: 他愿意为了莱斯莉付出一切。
t_en: He's willing to do anything for Lesley.

t_ch: …哼，也就是他那样一个地概科的愣头青、穷光蛋，才会血气上头，认为自己付得起所谓一切。
t_en: Hmph, only a Department of Cosmic Geography imbecile who lives in poverty would think he has nothing to lose.

t_ch: 也罢…要是我伯纳德的徒弟一点血性也没有，那才是丢尽了我的脸。
t_en: Fine... I would be ashamed if my student is a cold-blooded lizard.

t_ch: 我只是怕他所谓的付出一切，是要离开空间站，那我还是会有点…舍不得啊。
t_en: I'm just worried that when you said he's willing to do anything, it includes leaving the space station. I will... miss him.

t_ch: 他比我想象中要冲动。
t_en: He's even more impulsive than I thought.

t_ch: …哼，你不用说完我也知道，那小子八成早晚会离开空间站，寻找莱斯莉。
t_en: Hmph, I knew it. I knew that he'd leave the space station to look for Lesley.

t_ch: 只是事情真的发展到了这一步，对我这个老头来说，还是有点…舍不得。
t_en: But just when it actually happens, I think... I will miss him a lot.

t_ch: 毕竟，我这大半辈子的心血，除去倾注给地概科研究的那部分，就是给了洛奇这小子。
t_en: After all, other than Department of Cosmic Geography, I devoted the majority of my life to Rocky.

t_ch: 他或许会离开空间站…
t_en: He may leave the space station...

t_ch: …哼。其实我啊，也早就猜到了。
t_en: Hmph, I knew it from the very beginning.

t_ch: 只是…他好歹是我的徒弟，要我亲手促成他的离开，心中还是有许多不舍。
t_en: Just that... he's my student. I can't stand being the cause of his departure.

t_ch: 哈哈。有时候真是不知道，这股子不知天高地厚的冲劲，到底是他的优点，还是他的致命缺陷。
t_en: Haha, sometimes I really wonder if his naïve ambition is his virtue or weakness.

t_ch: 不过我想，既然这道关于爱情和莱斯莉的难题是由时间出予他作答的……
t_en: But I think it is "time" who threw this question at him, about love and Lesley...

t_ch: 那么，就由时间来揭晓终点的答案吧。
t_en: So, let's see what answer time will give him.

t_ch: 没想到你这年轻人，还会回来找我这个老头子。
t_en: I never thought you would visit me again, kid.

t_ch: 来把多余的备份还给你。
t_en: I'm here to return the other backup to you.

t_ch: 有始有终，是个可塑之才。多余的这一份，是红色转译录…看来，你选择向他隐瞒真相。
t_en: You are someone who finishes what you started. This is the red log... so you chose not to tell him the truth.

t_ch: 你也担心洛奇的情况吧…
t_en: You must be worried about Rocky...

t_ch: …我有什么好担心的！路是你们年轻人自己选的，遇到风险就得自己承担，我这种老头子，也只能在旁边干着急。
t_en: Not at all! You choose your own path and suffer your own consequences. An old man like me can do nothing but worry.

t_ch: 你手上剩下的是红色转译录…所以最后，你也骗了他。那小崽子，现在如何？
t_en: This is the red log... so in the end, you chose to lie to him. How is he doing?

t_ch: 他比我想象中要脆弱。
t_en: He's more fragile than I thought.

t_ch: …没错。你别看洛奇那小崽子平时大大咧咧，横冲直撞。他爱写诗，就说明心思比普通科员更为细腻。
t_en: Right. He may seem rough and direct, but the fact that he loves writing poetry shows his emotions are more delicate than the average researcher.

t_ch: 捧出一颗敏感的心，却被最为在意的心上人拒之门外——无疑是一种毁灭性的打击。听说，他把写的那些诗全撕了，这就是证明。
t_en: He gave her his sensitive heart but got rejected by someone he cares a lot about. It's undoubtedly devastating to him. I heard he tore up all the poems. That's the proof.

t_ch: 不过，从好的方面来看，你的选择也给了他一次磨练内心的机会。
t_en: However, on the bright side, your decision also gave him a chance to toughen up.

t_ch: 他撕毁了写的那些诗。
t_en: He tore up the poems he wrote.

t_ch: …是吗？这说明他受到的打击实在是不小，可惜了那些诗歌……
t_en: Did he? He must be devastated. I feel sorry for those poems...

t_ch: 小崽子，别用那种「你不嫌是酸诗吗」的怀疑目光看我。我虽然主张把研究精力用在正途上，但也不会漠视洛奇的心血。
t_en: Hey, what's that look on your face? I admit I did say they are "cringy," but I'd never ignore Rocky's efforts despite thinking think he should focus on his research.

t_ch: 那些诗歌，记录着他的心事。某种程度上，也是他人生的一部分。全撕了确实可惜，可叹。但是……
t_en: Those poems are records of his feelings. To some extent, they are part of his life. It's a shame that he tore them up. However...

t_ch: 他还能再振作起来吗？
t_en: Do you think he'll be able to pull himself together?

t_ch: 你的担心不无道理。我听说他把写的那些诗也全撕了…也好，就像有些科员失恋后也会选择剪去头发，象征新的开始。
t_en: Your concern is not groundless. I heard he's torn up all of his poems... Well, some researchers cut their hair after a breakup, symbolizing a new start.

t_ch: 多吃几顿好的，多睡几天大觉，把自己抛入研究工作的海洋，甚至找点不着调的缺德乐子…相信他早晚也能振作起来。
t_en: With a couple of good meals and a good rest, he'll throw himself back into his research, or even indulge himself in a random hobby... I'm sure he'll get over it sooner or later.

t_ch: 对年轻人来说，不论什么糟心事，那都是穿肠过的事。人生还长，这日子也总得过下去。
t_en: No matter how bad the situation is, young people can put it behind them in a blink of an eye. Life is long, we all have to live our lives.

t_ch: 有些实在无法遗忘的心结会伴随一生，直到我这把年纪，还无法释怀。那时候，这心结，也就成了一辈子的伙伴。
t_en: Some regrets will always stay in your life. Even at my age, there are regrets I can't let go of. By then, your regrets will become your life-long partner.

t_ch: 人总是需要伙伴…
t_en: Everyone needs a partner...

t_ch: 希望你也能找到忠于自己的伙伴，也希望这个伙伴，不是一件无法遗忘的憾事。
t_en: I hope you can find a partner who's loyal to you. And I hope this partner will never become a regret in your life.

t_ch: 这种伙伴不要也罢。
t_en: I don't want such a partner.

t_ch: 这样的想法很有魄力，可惜…大部分时候总是事与愿违。
t_en: That is a bold idea. Unfortunately... Things don't always go the way we want.

t_ch: 这话听起来有故事。
t_en: Seems there's a story behind it.

t_ch: 哈哈，没有什么故事，年轻人别想太多。
t_en: Haha, there isn't. Don't overthink it, young one.

t_ch: 对了，其实不是所有的诗歌都被洛奇给撕掉了……我这里还剩下一份。
t_en: By the way, Rocky didn't destroy all the poems... I kept one.

t_ch: 这是当他听我讲述完一对被时空阻隔的恋人的故事后，所创作的东西。
t_en: He wrote this after I told him a story about a couple who was separated by time and space.

t_ch: 就留给你，做个纪念吧。
t_en: Keep it. This is a souvenir for you.

t_ch: …必须时刻保持十二分的警惕，才能将星火燎原之前将其熄灭…
t_en: ...We must stay vigilant at all times if we are to prevent any potential disasters from getting out of hand...

t_ch: 打扰一下？
t_en: Excuse me?

t_ch: 防卫科负责人阿兰，向你报…啊，原来是{NICKNAME}。不好意思，没有第一时间认出你来。
t_en: Arlan, head of security, reporting to... Oh, it's you, {NICKNAME}. Sorry, I didn't recognize you at first.

t_ch: 我刚刚在思考怎么加强防卫科的灾患特训，毕竟这也是复兴计划的重要一环。「军团」胆敢再次来犯，别想再从我这讨到任何便宜！
t_en: I was just thinking about ways to improve the Security Department's emergency response training. After all, this is also an important part of Project Revival. If the Legion dares to attack us again, I don't want to make things easier for them!

t_ch: 复兴计划？
t_en: Project Revival?

t_ch: 嗯，复兴计划是艾丝妲小姐多方搜集空间站受损信息后，与五大科室负责人共同制定的一套灾后运转机制。
t_en: Well, Project Revival is a set of post-disaster operational protocols that Lady Asta and the heads of the five departments collaboratively developed, using data gathered about the damage sustained by the space station.

t_ch: 好，很有精神！
t_en: Wow, how proactive!

t_ch: 那是因为我非常相信艾丝妲小姐！
t_en: That's because I have a lot of faith in Lady Asta!

t_ch: 复兴计划可是她多方搜集空间站受损信息后，与五大科室负责人共同制定的一套灾后运转机制。
t_en: Project Revival is a set of post-disaster operational protocols that Miss Asta and the heads of the five departments collaboratively developed, using data gathered about the damage sustained by the space station.

t_ch: 其中还涉及许多其他机密概念，比如什么奇物再收容小队，安全区划立，「星际和平公司」定损之类的……
t_en: It also involves many other confidential initiatives, such as the Curio Collection Squad, security zoning, and the Interastral Peace Corporation's damage assessment.

t_ch: 奇物…什么来着？
t_en: Curio Collection... what?

t_ch: 奇物再收容小队，安全区划立，「星际和平公司」定损，这些都包含在复兴计划内。
t_en: Curio Collection Squad, security zoning, and the damage assessment of Interastral Peace Corporation are all part of Project Revival.

t_ch: 听起来很有条理。
t_en: Sounds very organized.

t_ch: 嗯，这都要归功于艾丝妲小姐和各科室负责人思路清晰，规划得当。
t_en: Well, it's all due to the clear thinking and proper planning by Lady Asta and the heads of the various departments.

t_ch: 但复兴计划实施起来并不容易，艾丝妲小姐也认为目前需要的人手超出了我们的预期……
t_en: But the implementation of Project Revival is not easy, and Lady Asta believes that the number of personnel needed exceeds our previous estimates.

t_ch: 虽然有些唐突，不过{NICKNAME}，你愿意帮忙吗？
t_en: Sorry if it seems to come out of nowhere, but {NICKNAME}, would you like to help?

t_ch: 之前与那帮「军团」怪物对阵的时候，{NICKNAME}你保护了我，是值得信赖的同伴。
t_en: You protected me in the past when we battled those Legion monsters, {NICKNAME}. You are truly a trustworthy comrade.

t_ch: 所以我第一时间想到了你。
t_en: That's why I immediately thought of seeking your help.

t_ch: 这是我的荣幸。
t_en: It would be my honor.

t_ch: 太好了！
t_en: Nice!

t_ch: 请随意差使我！
t_en: Please just order me around as you please!

t_ch: 怎么能说是差使…你愿意来帮忙，我们真的感激不尽。
t_en: This is not an "order" at all... If you are willing to help, we'd really appreciate it.

t_ch: 其实也不是什么棘手的事，科员们目前都在为复兴计划忙乎，我想拜托你到<color=#dbc291ff>空间站的各个舱段</color>看看他们是否需要<color=#dbc291ff>协助</color>。
t_en: It's nothing too difficult, really. The department staff are busy with Project Revival. Can you please go to <color=#dbc291ff>every zone in the space station</color> to see if they need <color=#dbc291ff>assistance</color>?

t_ch: 对了，这里是委托费，一共<unbreak>648</unbreak>…
t_en: By the way, here is your commission fee. A total of <unbreak>648</unbreak>...

t_ch: 我、我也不知道该给多少…我在这一直是包吃住的，对钱也没什么概念……
t_en: I—I'm not sure how much I should offer you... Since my living expenses are all already covered by the space station, I have no idea how much money is worth.

t_ch: 我一分不收。
t_en: I won't charge you anything.

t_ch: 请不要拒绝我……
t_en: Please don't turn down the offer...

t_ch: 多少都是情。
t_en: We can't put a price on our friendship.

t_ch: <unbreak>648</unbreak>是巨款！
t_en: <unbreak>648</unbreak> is a huge sum of money!

t_ch: 那么，就拜托你了！我向艾丝妲站长申请了「{NICKNAME}」专属的权限卡。
t_en: Well, we shall count on you, then. I will also ask the lead researcher, Asta, to issue an access card exclusively for you.

t_ch: 有{NICKNAME}协助复兴计划的稳步推进，希望艾丝妲小姐今后能多睡几个好觉……
t_en: Hopefully, with your help in Project Revival, Lady Asta will finally get a good night's sleep.

t_ch: 请、请放心，基座舱段的险况已经完全处于奇物再收容小队的控、控制中！
t_en: P—Please rest assured that this zone is now completely under the con—control of the Curio Collection Squad!

t_ch: 你的声音怎么在发抖？
t_en: Why is your voice shaking?

t_ch: 发、发抖？我没、没有，你一定是搞错了。
t_en: Shaking? It i—is not. You must've misheard.

t_ch: 呼，呼…镇定，亚伯拉罕。作为奇物再收容小队队员，必须时刻保持冷静。
t_en: Phew, phew... Calm down, Abraham. As a member of the Curio Collection Squad, you must always stay calm.

t_ch: 恐惧若被旁人察觉，不仅会失去信任，借机为阿尔弗瑞德报仇的愿望也会落空。
t_en: Fear, if picked up by others, will not only make everyone distrust you, but also ruin your chance to avenge Alfred.

t_ch: 我还记得你，怕死的伙计。
t_en: I remember you. You are that guy who is morbidly scared of death.

t_ch: 记得我？我、我也记得你…但我现在真的一点也不怕、怕死……
t_en: Remember me? I—I also remember you... But now I'm not a—afraid of death anymore...

t_ch: 与我朝夕相处的朋友已经率先步入了永夜，我的生命亦不知将在何时终结。
t_en: The friends who had once stood by my side day and night have already taken the lead in entering the eternal night, and yet I'm still here, not knowing when my life will end.

t_ch: 「人总是要死的，但不是现在。」
t_en: Life is but a path to death, but not today.

t_ch: 在为阿尔弗瑞德报仇之前，我还不想死在那些怪物手中！
t_en: I don't want to die at the hands of those monsters until I avenge Alfred!

t_ch: 辛克尔夫人把我从障碍判别终端调到了这里，让我跟着奇物再收容小队的温明德队长，在前线磨砺意志。
t_en: Mrs. Hinkel transferred me here from the Obstacle Detection Terminal so that I could follow the lead of Wen Mingde, head of the Curio Collection Squad, to toughen myself up on the front line.

t_ch: 但我一过来就碰到了件难事，就是、就是……
t_en: But as soon as I came here, I encountered a problem about, about...

t_ch: 重新激活那边的界域定锚！
t_en: Reactivating the Space Anchor over there!

t_ch: 这也太难了。
t_en: That's too hard.

t_ch: 你也这么觉得吧？！太好了，我不是一个人……
t_en: You think so too!? Great, I'm not alone.

t_ch: 连我都能做到。
t_en: Even I can do it.

t_ch: 可别这么说…你是拯救空间站的大英雄，这不是人人都能做到的事，谦虚不得……
t_en: Don't put it like that... You are the big hero who saved the space station. This's not something that anyone can do. You are being too modest...

t_ch: 就是几步路的事啊！
t_en: The solution is just a few steps away!

t_ch: 别看这几步路，要我鼓起毕生的勇气，也走不到……
t_en: It may look like a few steps, but I can't do it, even if I muster all my courage...

t_ch: 其实，激活本身不是难事，只需要走近界域定锚，你我的生物电场就会让它重新运转……
t_en: Well, actually, the activation itself isn't the difficult part. You just need to get close to the Space Anchor, and the biological polarity of your body will be enough to activate it.

t_ch: 阻挠我的，是盘踞于必经之路上的怪物。
t_en: The real challenge is all the monsters along the way.

t_ch: 以前在障碍判别终端，它们不过是远方的虚影，我的眼里只有蔑视。
t_en: Back when I was working at the Obstacle Detection Terminal, they were nothing but distant shadows that I could just brush aside.

t_ch: 直到这次入侵，我得知阿尔弗瑞德死于它们手中时，我的心中开始充斥着憎恨……
t_en: Then the invasion happened. And when I learned that they got Alfred, my heart began to fill up with hatred.

t_ch: 而当我成为奇物再收容小队一员，与它们面对面的时候，我却感到了——恐惧！
t_en: But when I became a member of the Curio Collection Squad and met them face to face, all I felt was fear!

t_ch: 那一瞬间，我的脑海中闪过阿尔弗瑞德那双逐渐涣散、黯淡的瞳孔。紧接着我呼吸急促，一身冷汗，双腿也打起颤来……
t_en: At that moment, Alfred's fading eyes flashed in my head. I felt short of breath, with cold sweat all over my body and my legs trembling...

t_ch: 很窝囊吧？无论如何在心里打气，我就是做不到激活界域定锚，还拖延了好几天…
t_en: What a loser, right? I just couldn't find the courage to go through with it and activate the Space Anchor. And it had been like this for several days...

t_ch: 所以，{NICKNAME}，能不能……
t_en: So, {NICKNAME}, do you think you can...

t_ch: 这事包在我身上！
t_en: Leave it to me!

t_ch: 我真的很钦佩你。你坚实的底气，向死而生的勇气，多少也感染了我。
t_en: I really admire you. Your perserverance and courage in the face of death have inspired me.

t_ch: 我也很清楚自己几斤几两，我会在这里等你的消息。
t_en: I'm well aware of my limitations, so I'll just wait here for an update from you.

t_ch: 一起去吧，有个照应。
t_en: Let's go together. We can look out for each other.

t_ch: 不不不，我不行的，一起去反而会帮倒忙……
t_en: N—N—No, I can't. I'll just get in the way...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，<color=#dbc291ff>激活界域定锚</color>这事就拜托你了！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, please <color=#dbc291ff>activate the Space Anchor</color>. I am counting on you!

t_ch: 左左左「巡猎」……
t_en: L—L—Left, The Hunt...

t_ch: 左「巡猎」，右「同谐」，「智识」在心间……
t_en: The Hunt on the left, Harmony on the right, and Erudition in the heart...

t_ch: 事情搞定了。
t_en: It's done.

t_ch: 好像有声音…但无论发生什么情况，即使在下一秒死去，我也得把口诀念完。
t_en: I can hear something... but no matter what happens, even if I die the next second, I must finish reciting this phrase.

t_ch: 左「巡猎」，右「同谐」，「智识」在心间，「存护」在胸前！
t_en: The Hunt on the left, Harmony on the right, Erudition in the heart, and Preservation on the chest!

t_ch: 好，做得很好，亚伯拉罕。
t_en: Okay, well done, Abraham.

t_ch: 咦，你这么快就回来啦？没想到这条鼓劲口诀我才念了<unbreak>167</unbreak>次，界域定锚就被激活了。
t_en: Wow, you are back already? I only recited this morale chant <unbreak>167</unbreak> times, and the Space Anchor is already activated.

t_ch: 你在干嘛呢？
t_en: What are you doing?

t_ch: 咦，是你回来啦。这么快？这条鼓劲口诀，我才念了<unbreak>167</unbreak>次而已……
t_en: Hey, you're back already! So soon! I've only recited this morale chant <unbreak>167</unbreak> times.

t_ch: 在你开口前，请允许我将它完整念完，否则，我会非常难受。
t_en: Before you say anything, please allow me to finish reciting it, otherwise, I will feel very uncomfortable.

t_ch: 我能理解！
t_en: I understand!

t_ch: 谢谢你，善良的人。
t_en: Thank you, you're very kind.

t_ch: 最好快点…
t_en: Well, hurry up...

t_ch: 嗯，我已经加快速度了。
t_en: Yes, I am going as fast as I can.

t_ch: 左「巡猎」，右「同谐」，「智识」在心间，「存护」在胸前！
t_en: The Hunt on the left, Harmony on the right, Erudition in the heart, and Preservation on the chest!

t_ch: 呼…好了。做得很好，亚伯拉罕。
t_en: Phew... Okay. Well done, Abraham.

t_ch: 现在，让我们回到正题吧，我看到那边的界域定锚已被重新激活。
t_en: Now, let's get back to business. I see that the Space Anchor over there has been reactivated.

t_ch: 盘龙在胸前！
t_en: The dragon on your chest!

t_ch: 是「存护」在胸前！
t_en: On the chest is Preservation!

t_ch: 这是我编纂的星神护心诀，可不能随便改词，以免冲撞了星神。
t_en: This is the chant of Aeon protection that I came up with myself. Please don't alter the wording, lest we offend the Aeons.

t_ch: 不过我没想到这条口诀才念了<unbreak>167</unbreak>次，你就激活了界域定锚。
t_en: But I only recited this chant <unbreak>167</unbreak> times and you already activated the Space Anchor.

t_ch: 其实刚刚念着鼓劲口诀，我也已经想明白了…既然{NICKNAME}都帮我完成了任务，让我还能留在小队中，我就一定要找出杀死阿尔弗瑞德的怪物，为他报仇。
t_en: Actually, when I was reciting the chant, I figured it all out... Since you've helped me complete my task already, I can now continue to stay on the team. And I will eventually hunt down the monster that killed Alfred and avenge him.

t_ch: 我会证明给你看，在死亡的长夜降临之前，我一定会勇敢地迈出那一步。
t_en: I will prove to you that I have the courage to take that step before the long night of death.

t_ch: 希望这不是你我的最后一面，有缘再会，{NICKNAME}……
t_en: I hope this is not our last encounter. Farewell, {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: 好事怎么没想起我…
t_en: How come you never have anything good for me?

t_ch: 啊？这难道不能算好事一桩吗……
t_en: Huh? Isn't this a good thing?

t_ch: 有了它，除开个别隐秘之地，<color=#dbc291ff>你就可以顺畅进出各舱室</color>。
t_en: With this, <color=#dbc291ff>you can travel between the various zones with ease</color>, except for certain hidden places.

t_ch: ——什么「智力竞赛为荣，体力消耗为耻」，都是杜撰！体力工作，难道就不是工作了吗？
t_en: ...What is this? "Battle of intellects is a source of pride. Physical exertion brings shame"? It's complete nonsense! So physical labor doesn't count as work now?

t_ch: 今天，我就要杀杀这坏风气。
t_en: Today, I'm gonna show them all what real work is about!

t_ch: 打、打扰了…
t_en: S—Sorry.

t_ch: 杀杀这坏风气！
t_en: Yep, show them!

t_ch: 我第一个赞成。
t_en: Hear! Hear!

t_ch: 在场所有科员都给我听好了——我们空间站「黑塔」，正处于艰难的复兴时期。
t_en: All staff present, attention please — Herta Space Station is undergoing a difficult period of revival.

t_ch: 与「军团」一役，朝夕相处的科员惨遭怪物屠戮，他们冰冷不瞑的身首被抛入寂静的寰宇……
t_en: In the battle with the Legion, some of our colleagues were slaughtered by monsters, and their cold bodies were thrown into the silent space.

t_ch: 数据入侵抹消了多年的研究心血；受损的奇物在无重力环境下缓慢崩解。
t_en: The cyber attack has erased years of research efforts. The damaged Curios are slowly disintegrating in a weightless environment.

t_ch: 目力所及之处都化为了「毁灭」的焦土，怪物余孽仍在银光闪烁的舱室内巡回。
t_en: Everything within sight has been turned into a wasteland, and monsters are still roaming around in our cabins.

t_ch: 无论你曾孤独地取得过多少耀眼的成绩，过往那种闭门造车的生活已经行不通了。
t_en: No matter how many dazzling achievements you have made on your own, we can no longer afford to live like hermits.

t_ch: 我们需要动员每一位科员的力量，放下固有的偏见，长期通力地合作，回收流散的奇物，修缮破损的舱室。
t_en: We need to mobilize every member of our staff, put down our inherent prejudices, and work together to recover the lost Curios and repair the damaged cabins.

t_ch: 有气势！
t_en: So inspiring!

t_ch: 好肉麻…
t_en: So lame...

t_ch: 安静！如果有科员心怀不满，请你当面向我反映。
t_en: Silence! If any staff member feels unhappy with this, please air your grievances to me face to face.

t_ch: 我学学。
t_en: I'm all ears.

t_ch: 如果各位没有异议，我不希望再听到任何对体力劳动的抱怨，也不想再看到任何工作推诿。
t_en: If you don't mind, I don't want to hear any more complaints about physical labor, nor do I want to see anyone putting off work.

t_ch: 有志于奇物回收工作的科员，请尽快到基座舱段的温明德队长手下的奇物再收容小队报道。
t_en: If you are interested in Curio recovery, please report to the Curio Collection Squad under Mr. Wen Mingde in the base zone as soon as possible.

t_ch: 有志于修缮工作的科员，现在就到我这里熟悉流程。
t_en: Those who are interested in repair work, come to me now to learn the process.

t_ch: 我来负责修缮工作。
t_en: I can help with the repairs.

t_ch: 好，很好。你是曾在主控舱段帮忙的年轻人？我有一项特殊任务要拜托你。
t_en: Good, good. Are you the young {M#man}{F#lady} who helped out in the master control zone? I have a special task for you.

t_ch: 每个人都得参与吗？
t_en: Does everyone have to participate?

t_ch: 每位科员早晚都要担起责任。空间站如今百废待兴，我相信，每个人都会有他的用武之处。
t_en: Every staff member must take responsibility sooner or later. The space station is now in dire need of restoration. I believe we can use everyone's help.

t_ch: 我记得你是曾在主控舱段帮忙的年轻人？那更好了，我有一项特殊任务要拜托你。
t_en: I remember you. You are the young {M#man}{F#lady} who helped out in the master control zone. Great. I have a special task for you.

t_ch: 距离你我现在所处位置，出门<unbreak>45</unbreak>米，有一处重要的道路缺损。
t_en: <unbreak>45</unbreak> meters out the door from where we are now, there is a major road defect.

t_ch: 我查探过，有怪物占领了缺损彼端的小型防卫库，普通科员恐怕无法解决。
t_en: I've checked, and the small ordnance depot on the other side of the defect is swarming with monsters. I'm afraid ordinary staff won't be able to deal with this.

t_ch: 放心交给我吧。
t_en: Don't worry. Just leave it to me.

t_ch: 看来只有我了…
t_en: Looks like I am the only one...

t_ch: 嗯，<color=#dbc291ff>务必修复道路缺损，清除扼守终点的怪物。</color>
t_en: Yes, <color=#dbc291ff>make sure to repair the road and eliminate the monsters at the end of it.</color>

t_ch: 我手上还有诸多事宜，请你完成任务后，向我汇报。
t_en: I have many other matters to attend to. Please report back to me when you finish the task.

t_ch: 年轻人，你回来了，嘘，不必多说。让我闭上眼，「检阅」你的工作。
t_en: {F#Young lady}{M#Young man}, you're back. Let me concentrate and inspect your work.

t_ch: 声源到达两耳的时间差在<unbreak>0.5</unbreak>毫秒左右，说明声源在三点或九点钟方向。
t_en: There is a <unbreak>0.5</unbreak> ms time delay between the sound reaching both ears, which indicates that the source is in the 3 or 9 o'clock position.

t_ch: 左耳声级大于右耳，确定在九点钟方向。说明三点钟方向的怪物已被清除。做得好。
t_en: The sound is much louder in the left ear, which confirms the 9 o'clock position. That means the monsters in the 3 o'clock position have been eliminated. Good job.

t_ch: 好厉害的技术！
t_en: Such an incredible skill!

t_ch: 简单的听声辩位而已，不值一提。
t_en: It's just sonar positioning. Nothing to brag about.

t_ch: 在我们「黑塔•星域界种科」，每一个人在跟银河生物打交道的过程中，都会磨练出这样的本领。
t_en: In the Herta Department of Ecology, we deal with all sorts of cosmic creatures every day. Developing this kind of ability is just part of our training.

t_ch: 「黑塔」科员本领各异，多接触一些，你就见怪不怪了。
t_en: The staff of Herta Space Station possess many different abilities. After meeting a few of us, you won't be surprised anymore.

t_ch: 我什么都没听见。
t_en: I can't hear anything.

t_ch: 你没受过训练，听不见也正常。这是空间站内界种科员独有的听声辩位技术。
t_en: You're untrained. It's normal that you can't hear anything. This is a sonar positioning technique that is unique to the space station's Ecology staff.

t_ch: 啊，也就是「黑塔•星域界种科」，其中每一个人在跟银河生物打交道的过程中，都会磨练出这样的本领。
t_en: In the Herta Department of Ecology, we deal with all sorts of cosmic creatures every day. Developing this kind of ability is just part of our training.

t_ch: 「黑塔」科员本领各异，多接触一些，你就见怪不怪了。
t_en: The staff of Herta Space Station possess many different abilities. After meeting a few of us, you won't be surprised anymore.

t_ch: 你就直接看一眼…
t_en: Why don't you just take a look...

t_ch: 呵呵，你说得也有道理。
t_en: Hehe, you have a point.

t_ch: 我也应该反思自己，是否也和众人一样，陷入了对「智识」的路径依赖，忽略了简单有效的身体力行。
t_en: I should also reflect on whether I, like so many others, have fallen into the trap of overreliance on Erudition and disregarding the much simpler and perhaps more effective methods.

t_ch: 总之，多谢你为「黑塔」做出的贡献。这是我向艾丝妲站长申请的奖励，请收下。
t_en: In short, thank you for your contribution to Herta Space Station. This is the reward I managed to secure for you from Lead Researcher Asta. Please take it.

t_ch: 谢谢，你对这里很上心。
t_en: Thank you. You seem to care a lot about this place.

t_ch: 一点小事，受之有愧。
t_en: No need to reward me for such a minor good deed.

t_ch: 不，正是一分一毫的小事，才支撑起了复兴的全局。
t_en: I disagree. All these minor acts of kindness are precisely what make the reconstruction possible.

t_ch: 当我还是一个籍籍无名的小科员时，第一批为建设「黑塔」耗尽心力的人曾告诉我，「这个宇宙并不友好，它是广阔而黑暗的。」
t_en: Back when I was an unknown researcher, I was told by the first group of people who worked on the construction of Herta Space Station, "This universe is never friendly. Rather, it is vast and dark."

t_ch: 在这幽茫宇宙中，有无数双眼睛在盯着「黑塔」，其目的各有不同。
t_en: In this vast universe, there are countless pairs of eyes staring at the space station, with various intentions.

t_ch: 有人觊觎它的奇物，有人嫉妒它的中立与自由。
t_en: Some covet its Curios, while others envy its neutrality and freedom.

t_ch: 也有人轻视它，把它当作可以随意破坏和践踏的棋子。
t_en: Others despise it or see it as a chess piece that can be destroyed and trampled on at will.

t_ch: 或许只有我们这群科员明白，它曾带给我们的喜悦、忧愁、顿悟与漫长的陪伴……
t_en: Perhaps we researchers are the only ones who understand the joy, sadness, epiphany, and companionship that it has brought us.

t_ch: 那么也将靠我们，为了保卫它而付出一切。
t_en: Which is why it is up to us to give everything we have to protect it.

t_ch: 温队长，打扰了…
t_en: Mr. Wen, excuse me...

t_ch: ——谁？甚么人在此骚扰？若是<color=#dbc291ff>水魈</color>冒替，还不速速现出原形！
t_en: ...Who? Who is interrupting me? If it is the <color=#dbc291ff>Xenohydro</color>, then show yourself at once!

t_ch: 咳，原来是{NICKNAME}，你也是来报名奇物收容志愿者…等等，你莫要过来！
t_en: Ahem, so it's you, {NICKNAME}. You're also here to volunteer in the Curio Collection Squad... Wait, don't come over here!

t_ch: 你先随意说个一二字。
t_en: Just say one or two words.

t_ch: 我不同你开玩笑，你必须说个一二字。
t_en: I'm not kidding. You have to say one and two.

t_ch: 一。
t_en: One.

t_ch: 一二。
t_en: One, two.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈。
t_en: Ahahaha.

t_ch: 倒也不必笑得如此夸张。
t_en: You don't have to laugh like that.

t_ch: 一。
t_en: One.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈。
t_en: Ahahaha.

t_ch: 倒也不必笑得如此夸张。
t_en: You don't have to laugh like that.

t_ch: …发音无异常水声，瞳孔反射正常，泌汗反应正常。
t_en: ... No abnormal watery sound in your pronunciation. Pupillary reflex normal, perspiration reaction normal.

t_ch: 好，现在你可过来一点，额头让我摸一下——
t_en: Okay, now you can come over and let me touch your forehead—

t_ch: 那<color=#dbc291ff>「异星水魈」</color>可做不到如此生动…哎，这千头万绪可又断喽。奇物收容作为「复兴宣言」的重中之重，实践起来却是难上加难。
t_en: The <color=#dbc291ff>Xenohydro</color> can't achieve this level of likeness... Alas, the lead was once again inconclusive. Even if the recollection of Curios is the top priority in the Declaration of Revival, it is proving to be a difficult task in practice.

t_ch: 异星水魈？
t_en: Xenohydro?

t_ch: 你也知道，自「黑塔」建站以来，围绕奇物收容就设立了<color=#dbc291ff>界种、法政、密卷、地概、应物</color>五个科室。
t_en: As you know, since the establishment of the Herta Space Station, five departments have been established to focus on the collection and study of Curios, including <color=#dbc291ff>Ecology, Geopolitics, Mysticism, Geography, and Implement Arts</color>.

t_ch: 每个科室都珍藏着数以百计的藏品。由于此次怪物入侵，约莫有大半都在冲击中流散。
t_en: This monster invasion caused the loss of roughly half of the hundreds of collections held by each department.

t_ch: 它们形貌各异，形状不一。有的大，有的小，有的静置无害，有的却无端流毒，更别提其中还有一些能跑能跳的生物型藏品，令人殊为头痛……
t_en: They come in all kinds of sizes and shapes. Some are inactive and harmless, some emit poison for no reason, not to mention some biological types of Curios that can plan their own escape, which is a real headache.

t_ch: 有一种名为<color=#dbc291ff>「异星水魈」</color>的奇物，可以任意幻化成他人相貌，顶着各重假身份，到处为非作歹。
t_en: There is a Curio called the <color=#dbc291ff>Xenohydro</color> that has the ability to replicate the appearance of other people at will. This allows it to wreak havoc and confusion wherever it goes, as it can easily assume various false identities.

t_ch: 奇物收容？
t_en: Curio Collection?

t_ch: 对，就是那个我组建的奇物再收容小队嘛，是空间站依据灾后复兴需要，临时牵头成立的应急小组。
t_en: The Curio Collection Squad is an emergency team I put together. The team's mission is to support post-disaster reconstruction and ensure that all of its needs are met during this difficult time.

t_ch: 顾名思义，主要负责回收因怪物入侵而收容失效的奇物。
t_en: As the name suggests, it is mainly responsible for collecting the Curios that have been displaced due to the recent monster invasion.

t_ch: 复兴宣言？
t_en: Declaration of Revival?

t_ch: 辛克尔夫人那段著名的「复兴宣言」，你想必也早已有所耳闻吧。
t_en: Surely, you must have heard of Mrs. Hinkel's famous Declaration of Revival, right?

t_ch: 「朝夕相处的科员惨遭怪物屠戮，他们冰冷不瞑的…」
t_en: "Our fellow researchers were slaughtered by monsters. Their lifeless forms were cold and woeful..."
